text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT
Department of Anatomic Pathology

PATIENT: Margaret Osei
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
SURGEON: [REDACTED]
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Gynecologic Oncology
PROCEDURE: Primary Debulking Surgery — Total Abdominal Hysterectomy, Bilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy, Infracolic Omentectomy, Appendectomy, Bilateral Pelvic Peritoneal Stripping, Right Hemidiaphragm Peritonectomy, Pelvic and Para-aortic Lymph Node Sampling
CLINICAL HISTORY: 52-year-old postmenopausal female with newly diagnosed high-grade serous carcinoma, FIGO Stage IIIC, confirmed on prior omental biopsy. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC) identified. Presenting CA-125 1,847 U/mL. Presenting for planned primary debulking surgery with intent of R0 resection. Prior diagnostic omental biopsy confirmed HGSC with WT1+, PAX8+, p53 aberrant, Ki-67 ~70%.

---

SPECIMENS SUBMITTED

Specimen A: Right ovary and fallopian tube (en bloc)
Specimen B: Left ovary and fallopian tube (en bloc)
Specimen C: Uterus with attached parametrial tissue and upper vaginal cuff
Specimen D: Infracolic omentum
Specimen E: Appendix
Specimen F: Bilateral pelvic peritoneum (multiple fragments)
Specimen G: Right hemidiaphragm peritoneum (stripped)
Specimen H: Right pelvic lymph nodes (submitted in separate containers by station)
Specimen I: Left pelvic lymph nodes
Specimen J: Para-aortic lymph nodes (infrarenal)

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Specimen A — Right Ovary and Fallopian Tube:
Received fresh, labeled "right ovary and fallopian tube." The ovary measures 7.2 x 5.8 x 4.9 cm and is largely replaced by a solid and cystic neoplasm. The external surface shows extensive tumor studding with multiple tan-white nodular excrescences and areas of hemorrhagic discoloration consistent with capsular rupture (noted intraoperatively). On sectioning, the cut surface reveals a heterogeneous tan-yellow to gray-white solid mass with focal areas of hemorrhage and necrosis; no normal ovarian parenchyma is identifiable. The adherent right fallopian tube measures 7.5 cm in length and 0.6 cm in greatest diameter. The fimbriated end is identifiable; no gross tumor is seen within the tubal lumen. Representative sections submitted in cassettes A1–A8 (including fallopian tube fimbriated end in entirety per SEE-FIM protocol).

Specimen B — Left Ovary and Fallopian Tube:
Received fresh, labeled "left ovary and fallopian tube." The ovary measures 5.8 x 4.4 x 3.6 cm and is similarly replaced by a solid-cystic mass. External surface demonstrates focal surface tumor involvement without obvious capsular rupture grossly, though microscopic assessment is definitive. Cut surface reveals a tan-white to grayish-pink solid mass with focal necrosis and rare small cystic spaces containing clear to sanguinous fluid. The left fallopian tube measures 6.8 cm in length and 0.7 cm in diameter. The fimbriated end is submitted in entirety per SEE-FIM protocol. On gross examination, the fimbriated end appears slightly thickened and granular. Cassettes B1–B8.

Specimen C — Uterus with Parametria and Upper Vaginal Cuff:
Received fresh in the open state, labeled "uterus." Total specimen weight 82 grams. The uterus measures 8.5 x 4.8 x 3.2 cm. The serosal surface is smooth and glistening without obvious tumor implants. On serial sectioning, the endometrium is thin (0.2 cm), atrophic in appearance consistent with postmenopausal status. The myometrium is uniformly tan-pink and measures up to 1.5 cm in thickness. No intramural or submucosal masses. Cervix measures 3.2 x 2.8 cm with a patent external os; ectocervix appears smooth and tan-pink; no polyps or lesions. Upper vaginal cuff margins are inked. Cassettes C1–C10 (full myometrial sections, cervical sections including transformation zone, and vaginal cuff margin).

Specimen D — Infracolic Omentum:
Received fresh, labeled "omentum." The omentum is a large sheet of fibrofatty tissue measuring 35 x 22 x up to 5 cm in greatest dimensions. The tissue is largely replaced and distorted by confluent, firm tan-white nodular tumor deposits — so-called "omental caking." On sectioning, the cut surfaces show near-confluent replacement of the omental adipose tissue by gray-white carcinoma with central areas of geographic necrosis. Multiple sections submitted to document extent of involvement. Representative sections in cassettes D1–D12.

Specimen E — Appendix:
Received fixed, labeled "appendix." The appendix measures 5.8 cm in length and 0.6 cm in external diameter. The serosal surface is smooth and pink-tan without tumor nodules. The lumen is patent and contains no obstructing lesion. The wall thickness and mucosa appear unremarkable grossly. Tip and base sections submitted in cassettes E1–E2.

Specimen F — Bilateral Pelvic Peritoneum:
Received as multiple irregular fragments of peritoneal tissue in aggregate measuring 18 x 12 x up to 1.2 cm. Multiple white-tan nodular deposits visible on the peritoneal surface, ranging from 0.3 to 1.8 cm. All fragments submitted entirely. Cassettes F1–F8.

Specimen G — Right Hemidiaphragm Peritoneum (Stripped):
Received as multiple irregular strips of peritoneum measuring in aggregate 14 x 7 x up to 0.5 cm. Tan-white nodular deposits visible on the mesothelial surface, the largest measuring 0.9 cm. Entire specimen submitted. Cassettes G1–G4.

Specimen H — Right Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
Received in multiple labeled station containers. Aggregate fibrofatty tissue. A total of 9 lymph nodes are identified, ranging from 0.4 to 1.6 cm. All lymph nodes submitted entirely or bisected. Cassettes H1–H5.

Specimen I — Left Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
Aggregate fibrofatty tissue. A total of 7 lymph nodes are identified, ranging from 0.3 to 1.4 cm. All submitted. Cassettes I1–I4.

Specimen J — Para-aortic Lymph Nodes (Infrarenal):
Aggregate fibrofatty tissue. A total of 11 lymph nodes are identified, ranging from 0.4 to 2.1 cm. All submitted entirely or bisected. Cassettes J1–J6.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS

Specimen A — Right Ovary and Fallopian Tube:
Sections of the right ovary show near-total replacement of the ovarian parenchyma by a high-grade epithelial malignancy. The tumor displays architectural patterns characteristic of high-grade serous carcinoma: solid sheets, nests, papillary structures, and slit-like spaces lined by markedly atypical epithelial cells. The neoplastic cells are large with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, pleomorphic vesicular nuclei, macronucleoli, and brisk mitotic activity (readily exceeding 10 mitotic figures per high-power field). Atypical mitotic figures are identified. Areas of geographic necrosis are present. Psammoma bodies are focally present. Lymphovascular space invasion (LVSI) is identified. The tumor extends through the ovarian capsule with surface involvement confirmed microscopically (capsular rupture present). Sections of the right fallopian tube show a benign epithelial lining with no evidence of serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC) or invasive carcinoma.

Diagnosis — Specimen A: HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA extensively involving the right ovary, with capsular rupture and surface involvement. Right fallopian tube: no malignancy identified.

Specimen B — Left Ovary and Fallopian Tube:
Sections of the left ovary show replacement by the same high-grade serous carcinoma as described in Specimen A, with solid and papillary growth patterns, marked nuclear pleomorphism, brisk mitosis, and focal necrosis. Surface involvement is present microscopically. Sections through the entirety of the left fallopian tube fimbriated end (SEE-FIM protocol) reveal a discrete focus of markedly atypical epithelial cells confined to the tubal epithelium at the fimbriated end, demonstrating loss of polarity, nuclear stratification, and strong aberrant p53 expression (see ancillary studies), consistent with SEROUS TUBAL INTRAEPITHELIAL CARCINOMA (STIC). This finding supports the fimbriated end of the left fallopian tube as the most probable site of tumor origin. No invasive carcinoma in the fallopian tube proper.

Diagnosis — Specimen B: HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA extensively involving the left ovary with surface involvement; SEROUS TUBAL INTRAEPITHELIAL CARCINOMA (STIC) of the left fallopian tube fimbriated end.

Specimen C — Uterus, Parametria, Vaginal Cuff:
Sections of the endometrium show atrophic endometrium with inactive glands; no hyperplasia, no in-situ or invasive carcinoma. The myometrium shows no evidence of invasion. Cervical sections including transformation zone show benign squamocolumnar junction; no cervical intraepithelial neoplasia (CIN); no invasive carcinoma. Uterine serosa is free of tumor. Vaginal cuff margins are negative for malignancy. Parametrial soft tissue: no carcinoma identified.

Diagnosis — Specimen C: Atrophic endometrium and benign cervix; no malignancy identified. Surgical margins negative.

Specimen D — Omentum:
Sections demonstrate extensive involvement of the omental adipose tissue by high-grade serous carcinoma, consistent with "omental caking" noted grossly. The carcinoma replaces the vast majority of the omental parenchyma, with infiltration of adipose tissue, fibrous stroma, and lymphovascular spaces. Central areas of tumor necrosis are seen. Psammoma bodies focally present.

Diagnosis — Specimen D: Omentum extensively involved by high-grade serous carcinoma.

Specimen E — Appendix:
Sections show a normal vermiform appendix with intact mucosa, submucosa, muscularis propria, and serosa. No carcinoma or other neoplasm identified. No carcinoid/neuroendocrine tumor. The periappendiceal soft tissue is free of tumor.

Diagnosis — Specimen E: Appendix with no pathologic abnormality; no malignancy.

Specimen F — Bilateral Pelvic Peritoneum:
Sections show fibrofatty and fibrovascular peritoneal tissue with multiple deposits of high-grade serous carcinoma on the peritoneal surface and in the subperitoneal adipose tissue. The largest implant measures 1.8 cm in greatest dimension microscopically. Peritoneal margins (inked tissue edges) are negative for carcinoma.

Diagnosis — Specimen F: Peritoneum with multiple high-grade serous carcinoma implants; excision margins negative.

Specimen G — Right Hemidiaphragm Peritoneum:
Sections of the stripped diaphragmatic peritoneum show fibroelastic tissue with tumor implants of high-grade serous carcinoma present on the mesothelial surface, the largest measuring 0.9 cm. The deep resection margins are negative for carcinoma (no full-thickness diaphragmatic involvement).

Diagnosis — Specimen G: Right hemidiaphragm peritoneum with high-grade serous carcinoma implants; deep margins negative.

Specimen H — Right Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
Sections of 9 lymph nodes show reactive follicular and paracortical hyperplasia. No evidence of metastatic carcinoma in any of the 9 lymph nodes examined. No isolated tumor cells by H&E. Extranodal extension: not applicable.

Diagnosis — Specimen H: Nine (9) lymph nodes, negative for metastatic carcinoma (0/9).

Specimen I — Left Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
Sections of 7 lymph nodes show sinus histiocytosis and mild reactive changes. No metastatic carcinoma.

Diagnosis — Specimen I: Seven (7) lymph nodes, negative for metastatic carcinoma (0/7).

Specimen J — Para-aortic Lymph Nodes (Infrarenal):
Sections of 11 lymph nodes show mild reactive changes, including scattered germinal centers. No evidence of metastatic carcinoma in any of the 11 lymph nodes examined. No isolated tumor cells.

Diagnosis — Specimen J: Eleven (11) para-aortic lymph nodes, negative for metastatic carcinoma (0/11).

Total lymph nodes examined: 27 (pelvic: 16; para-aortic: 11)
Total lymph nodes positive: 0

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

Immunohistochemistry (performed on Specimens A, B, and D; results concordant across specimens):

- WT1: Positive, diffuse strong nuclear staining in tumor cells
- PAX8: Positive, diffuse nuclear staining
- p53: Aberrant pattern — diffuse strong ("block") nuclear positivity in >90% of tumor cells, consistent with missense TP53 gain-of-function mutation (concordant with TP53 p.R248W identified on prior molecular testing)
- CK7: Positive, diffuse
- CK20: Negative
- Estrogen Receptor (ER): Focally positive (approximately 15% of tumor nuclei, 1+ intensity)
- Progesterone Receptor (PR): Negative
- BRCA1 protein (by IHC, clone MS110): Absent/lost nuclear expression in tumor cells; positive internal control (stromal cells retain staining). Findings consistent with biallelic BRCA1 inactivation in the context of known germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant.
- Ki-67 proliferation index: Approximately 65–70% (consistent with high-grade designation)
- p16: Diffuse strong block positivity (nonspecific in this context; included per institutional panel)
- Napsin A: Negative (supports non-pulmonary primary)
- CDX2: Negative

Note: STIC focus in left fallopian tube fimbriated end shows identical p53 aberrant (diffuse block) staining pattern and strong WT1 and PAX8 co-expression, supporting the contiguous nature of this lesion with the primary ovarian/peritoneal disease.

---

FINAL DIAGNOSES (SUMMARY)

1. RIGHT OVARY: High-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC), 7.2 cm, with capsular rupture and ovarian surface involvement. LVSI present.
   Right fallopian tube: No malignancy.

2. LEFT OVARY: High-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC), 5.8 cm, with ovarian surface involvement.
   Left fallopian tube: Serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC) of the fimbriated end — probable site of origin.

3. UTERUS / CERVIX / VAGINAL CUFF / PARAMETRIA: No malignancy. All surgical margins negative.

4. OMENTUM: Extensively involved by high-grade serous carcinoma (omental caking).

5. APPENDIX: No malignancy.

6. BILATERAL PELVIC PERITONEUM: Multiple high-grade serous carcinoma implants (largest 1.8 cm); excision margins negative.

7. RIGHT HEMIDIAPHRAGM PERITONEUM: High-grade serous carcinoma implants present; deep margins negative.

8. PELVIC LYMPH NODES (RIGHT + LEFT): 0/16 positive for metastatic carcinoma.

9. PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES (INFRARENAL): 0/11 positive for metastatic carcinoma.

OVERALL LYMPH NODE STATUS: 0/27 lymph nodes positive.

---

PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition / FIGO 2014)

pT: pT3c — Tumor involving one or both ovaries with macroscopic peritoneal metastases beyond the pelvis >2 cm in greatest dimension; diaphragmatic implants present
pN: pN0 — No regional lymph node metastasis (27 lymph nodes examined, all negative)
pM: Not applicable (no distant organ parenchymal metastasis identified in resected specimens)

FINAL PATHOLOGIC STAGE: pT3c pN0 pMX
FIGO STAGE: IIIC

Residual Disease Status (per surgical report and gross/microscopic assessment of all margins): R0 — No residual macroscopic or microscopic tumor at any surgical margin. Complete gross resection achieved.

---

ADDITIONAL COMMENTS

1. The identification of STIC at the left fallopian tube fimbriated end, with morphologic and immunophenotypic features identical to the ovarian carcinoma, supports the current model of high-grade serous carcinoma arising from the fallopian tube epithelium, consistent with BRCA1-associated tumorigenesis.

2. Loss of BRCA1 protein expression by IHC in tumor cells, in the context of the known germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC), is consistent with biallelic inactivation and loss of homologous recombination proficiency.

3. All specimens were processed and stained per College of American Pathologists (CAP) checklist requirements for ovarian/fallopian tube/peritoneal carcinoma.

---

SIGNED:
Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED], MD
Department of Surgical Pathology

Case reviewed and signed out by: [REDACTED]
Report finalized: [DATE REDACTED]
